page: Microsoft Azure E2pds_v5 is an ARM64-based virtual machine belonging to the EPDSv5 server family. Powered by a 3.0 GHz Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor, this instance features 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs with a single thread per core. The memory configuration consists of 16.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Storage is bundled with a 77 GB local disk, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. The underlying virtualization is managed by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks indicate moderate single-core capabilities but overall constrained multi-core and memory throughput. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance falls in the average range at 1821.79, and single-threaded gzip compression performs moderately at 24.19 GB/sec. However, multi-core stress-ng CPU performance and Geekbench multi-core scores are positioned in the lower tiers. Memory bandwidth tests show 140.76 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 24.9 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Database operations, such as Redis SET operations, achieve 2.8 million operations per second, while static web serving throughput reaches 2.76 GB/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the E2pds_v5 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio on an energy-efficient ARM architecture, making it a resource-dense option for memory-bound workloads that do not require intensive parallel computation. The lack of advanced vector or matrix extensions and lower multi-threaded performance limit its suitability for heavy mathematical modeling, ray tracing, or large-scale machine learning. Instead, this server is best suited for lightweight databases, caching layers, memory-intensive web serving, and general-purpose ARM64 development or testing environments.
description: The Microsoft Azure E2pds_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine from the EPDSv5 family, powered by a 3.0 GHz Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM. This configuration provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core, complemented by a 77 GB local disk and Premium Storage capabilities. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the instance demonstrates average single-core performance in stress-ng and gzip compression, though its multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics fall into lower performance tiers. Lacking AVX and AMX extensions, it is not designed for high-performance computing or complex vector math. Instead, the E2pds_v5 represents a qualitatively cost-efficient, memory-optimized option for workloads requiring a larger memory footprint relative to compute power, such as lightweight databases, caching layers, static web serving, and general-purpose ARM64 application hosting.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E2pds_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated Ampere Neoverse-N1 vCPUs, 16 GB of RAM, and a 77 GB local disk, optimized for memory-intensive workloads requiring moderate compute power.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E2pds_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine with 2 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 77 GB local disk, designed for memory-optimized workloads.
tagline: An ARM64-powered virtual machine offering a high memory-to-core ratio and local storage for memory-intensive, lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: ARM64 architecture powered by a 3.0 GHz Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor
2 dedicated vCPUs paired with 16.0 GB of RAM (8.0 GB per core)
Includes 77 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage
Average single-core CPU and gzip compression performance
Best suited for lightweight databases, caching, and ARM64 development
